The genus Peperomia is further divided into several species, with Peperomia tenera being one of the many species within this group. The classification of Peperomia tenera is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf shape, flower structure, and growth habits.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Peperomia tenera include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
